symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral symmetrical shoulder- and pelvic-girdle pain and stiffness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral proximal girdle involvement is the defining feature of PMR.
Mechanical OA is typically asymmetrical and activity-worsened, not acute bilateral girdle stiffness worst after rest.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inactivity/morning stiffness (gel phenomenon) eased by warmth and gentle movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged inactivity stiffness relieved by warmth/movement is an inflammatory (not mechanical) pattern typical of PMR.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe exacerbation after prolonged rest in a cold room

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cold and immobility provoke inflammatory girdle stiffness, producing the acute 'locked-up' exacerbation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain-limited reduced shoulder abduction and inability to rise from a chair without arm assistance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Movement limited by proximal pain (not by true weakness) fits PMR; helps distinguish it from myositis.
Myositis causes true painless-to-mildly-painful weakness; here movement is limited by pain with preserved power.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal reported distal strength and sensation, no numbness, no bladder/bowel dysfunction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact distal power, sensation and continence make an acute cord compression far less likely (though not fully excluded remotely).

symptoms.symptom_name: Distal muscle power 5/5 (grip, ankle dorsiflexion), no upper-motor-neuron signs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective neurological examination is what definitively excludes cord compression — a clinician measurement sealed from the AI Doctor, obtainable only in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative GCA screen — no visual change, temporal headache, scalp tenderness or jaw claudication

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of cranial GCA features makes active GCA unlikely now, but the association mandates ongoing safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: No trauma or fall; bilateral symmetrical onset without mechanism

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An atraumatic, bilateral, symmetrical onset is inconsistent with an occult fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal temporal artery palpation and cranial-nerve examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-tender, normal temporal arteries reduce GCA likelihood — a clinician examination not available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Peripheral joints (wrists, knees) without effusion or warmth

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of peripheral synovitis or a hot joint argues against a peripheral inflammatory or septic arthropathy — requires in-person joint examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep tenderness over deltoid, trapezius and gluteal muscle groups

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Proximal periarticular/muscular tenderness fits PMR — a palpation finding sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Borderline ESR/CRP elevation on recent outpatient labs

investigation_details.expected_result: borderline / mildly raised (atypical for classic PMR, consistent with the mild presentation)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: No objective inflammatory baseline before steroids; harder to confirm the diagnosis and monitor response.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised inflammatory markers support PMR; borderline values fit the mild/atypical variant — a lab result unavailable in real time via telehealth.
Absence of fever or systemic illness reduces the likelihood of infection driving the presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals normal/stable (BP 145/85, HR 90, afebrile, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haemodynamic stability and no fever are reassuring against sepsis/shock but are non-specific; these are clinician-measured and not obtainable via chat without a home device.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bilateral girdle pain with inactivity stiffness, pain-limited movement, proximal tenderness and raised inflammatory markers in an older adult is the PMR constellation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline inflammatory markers before commencing steroids; support the PMR diagnosis and provide a monitoring baseline.
Normal in PMR; a markedly raised CK would point to inflammatory myositis, given the weakness-like presentation.
Excludes hypothyroid myopathy as a mimic of proximal symptoms.
Baseline safety bloods before corticosteroids in an older adult (renal function, glucose, blood count).
GCA is screened negative; do NOT order routinely — reserve for patients with cranial GCA features.
No trauma and a bilateral symmetrical onset make occult fracture unlikely; routine imaging is over-investigation. Image only if focal red flags emerge on in-person examination.
